meaning: n.[植](新西兰产红花银叶的)桃金娘科常绿树
examples: You can lie on the lawn beneath the ancient pohutukawa.
Multiple outdoor living areas mean you can be poolside, on the lawn beneath the ancient pohutukawa, or in front of the fire.
Plants fight for space: ponga (silver tree fern), cabbage trees, manuka (tea tree), and pohutukawa (a coastal evergreen tree), which cling to the land at the sea's edge.
